paymentModel: Paystack platform: one platform merchant, a subaccount per business created server-side from (bankCode, accountNumber, accountName) — nothing else stored.

fields.name: code
fields.note: NUBAN/Paystack bank code, e.g. '044', '50191', '999992'. Tuple slot .$1 in const kBanks.

fields.name: name
fields.note: Human bank name, e.g. 'Access Bank'. Tuple slot .$2 in const kBanks.

mockRef.evidence: const kBanks = <(String, String)> of 19 Nigerian banks with NUBAN codes; doc comment line 22 'Mock of GET /banks until the backend proxies Paystack's list.' Real endpoint proxies Paystack's bank list.

payload.name: accountNumber
payload.note: 10-digit NUBAN, digits-only; UI enforces length==10 before firing (LengthLimitingTextInputFormatter(10), digitsOnly)

fields.name: accountName
fields.note: bank-registered account holder name (Paystack name lookup). Displayed uppercased as 'NAME — is this you?'

mockRef.evidence: Fires on (accountNumber digits==10 && bank!=null). Mock: 700ms delay then reads repo.getBusiness() and returns ownerName.toUpperCase() (fallback 'ACCOUNT HOLDER'). Comment lines 81-82: 'the wired version calls POST /payout-account/resolve (Paystack account lookup) and shows the bank's registered name.'

payload.name: accountName
payload.note: the resolved name confirmed by the owner (from resolveAccount response)

expectedResponse.note: Server creates/updates the business's Paystack subaccount from these three fields; nothing else stored.

mockRef.evidence: As-built persists to shared_preferences via appPrefs.setString: 'payout.bankCode', 'payout.bankName', 'payout.accountNumber', 'payout.accountName' — a backend seam, NOT the repo/drift DB. Save enabled only when bank!=null && resolvedName!=null && !resolving (line 173). Note: bankName is a client-side convenience pref only; per conventions the wire payload is bankCode+accountNumber+accountName only.

authContext.note: Payout account is owner-only per conventions (Roles & permissions: staff cannot touch billing/payout/subscription). Enforce @Roles('owner').

uiStatesDetail.evidence: No account number / <10 digits or no bank selected: _resolvedName cleared, save button disabled (lines 71-79, 173).
_resolvedName!=null shows green confirmation '$name — is this you?' (lines 279-308); enables Save.

Not rendered in as-built mock (resolve cannot fail locally), but the wired POST /payout-account/resolve must surface a failed/invalid-account state (Paystack lookup can reject). Backend contract returns error envelope; UI to handle resolve failure.

notes: Screen reads repo.getBusiness() only to fabricate the mock resolved name (ownerName); this is a mock stand-in for the Paystack name lookup, not a real read contract.

PaystackLinkService (lib/data/payments/paystack_links.dart) is a sibling Paystack seam for debtor pay-links (createPayLink with subaccount, webhook reconcile) — not exercised by this screen's save/resolve, but confirms the subaccount-per-business model this screen provisions.
Money (S-1 kobo) and UUIDv7 ids (S-2) do not apply to these three payload shapes — no money or entity id fields are on the wire here.
